name: terminal-activity-markers
displayName: Terminal Activity Monitor for VS Code
description: Monitors live, completed, and recent terminal activity in VS Code's native terminal list.

commands.title: Terminal Activity Monitor: Refresh Native Terminal Names
Terminal Activity Monitor: Disable and Clear Native Markers

terminalActivityDashboard.activeAfterHou.description: Keep a terminal green for this many hours after creation or detected output, CPU, and shell-command activity. Terminal selection and idle process existence do not count as activity.

terminalActivityDashboard.parkedAfterHou.description: Turn a terminal's dot from yellow to white after this many hours without activity.

terminalActivityDashboard.refreshInterva.description: How often native terminal activity dots are refreshed.

terminalActivityDashboard.liveIndicatorS.description: Show a 🟢🟢 marker for this many seconds after output, CPU, or shell-command activity is detected. Set to 0 to disable the live marker.

terminalActivityDashboard.completionMark.description: Show ✅ or ❌ when a qualifying shell-integrated command finishes in a terminal that is not selected. Selecting the terminal acknowledges the marker.

terminalActivityDashboard.completionMini.description: Only show completion markers for shell commands that ran for at least this many seconds.

terminalActivityDashboard.nativeNameMark.description: Automatically prefix native terminal names with live, completion, and age markers. Names update when commands run and activity thresholds are crossed. This can momentarily focus terminals because of VS Code API limitations.

terminalActivityDashboard.renameExisting.description: Also apply native name markers to terminals that were already open before the extension activated.
